    26→## 1. System Overview
    27→
    28→```
    29→┌──────────────┐     ┌───────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐
    30→│  Web browser │────▶│  nginx / CDN  │────▶│  Go API      │
    31→│  Mobile app  │     │  (static +    │     │  (:8081)     │
    32→│              │     │   reverse     │     │  GraphQL     │
    33→│  AI agent    │────▶│   proxy)      │     │  MCP         │
    34→│  (MCP)       │     └───────────────┘     │  REST        │
    35→└──────────────┘                           └──────┬───────┘
    36→                                                  │
    37→                                                  ▼
    38→                                           ┌──────────────┐
    39→                                           │ PostgreSQL   │
    40→                                           │ 15 + PostGIS │
    41→                                           └──────────────┘
    42→```
    43→
    44→### Monorepo layout
    45→
    46→```
    47→kapow/
    48→├── api/                  # Go API server
    49→│   ├── cmd/server/       # main.go entrypoint
    50→│   ├── graph/            # GraphQL: schema SDL, generated code, resolvers, scalars
    51→│   ├── internal/         # auth, config, db, handler, middleware, mcp, router, search, server, testdb, version
    52→│   ├── migrations/       # Goose SQL + embed wrapper
    53→│   └── gqlgen.yml
    54→├── web/                  # React 18 + Vite + TypeScript
    55→│   ├── src/
    56→│   │   ├── components/   # layout/, ui/, search/
    57→│   │   ├── pages/        # Home, Search, About
    58→│   │   ├── hooks/        # useWebMCP
    59→│   │   └── test/         # Vitest setup, helpers
    60→│   ├── demo/             # Playwright walkthrough
    61→│   └── schema.graphql    # Generated from API SDL
    62→├── common/               # Shared schema + fixture data
    63→├── tools/                # Developer tools (samplesql)
    64→├── docker/               # nginx, supervisord, entrypoint
    65→├── scripts/              # dev.sh, gen-schema.sh, smoke tests
    66→├── Dockerfile            # All-in-one test image
    67→└── Makefile              # Root — delegates to api/ and web/
    68→```
    69→
    70→### Technology choices
    71→
    72→| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
    73→|---|---|---|
    74→| API language | Go 1.23+ | Strong stdlib, single binary, excellent concurrency, fast compilation |
    75→| HTTP router | chi/v5 | stdlib-compatible, composable middleware, lightweight |
    76→| GraphQL | gqlgen (schema-first) | Type-safe codegen, SDL is the source of truth, good Go integration |
    77→| Database | PostgreSQL 15+ with PostGIS | Mature, JSONB flexibility, full-text search, geographic queries, materialized views |
    78→| DB driver | pgx/v5 + pgxpool | Native PostgreSQL protocol, connection pooling, context-aware |
    79→| Migrations | goose/v3 (embedded) | SQL-based, embedded in binary via go:embed, up/down support |
    80→| Auth | golang-jwt/v5 | HS256 + RS256, standard JWT claims |
    81→| Config | caarlos0/env | Struct-tag binding from env vars, zero config files |
    82→| Logging | stdlib log/slog | Structured, leveled, JSON or text output |
    83→| Frontend | React 18 + TypeScript 5 + Vite 5 | Fast HMR, strict types, tree-shaking, native ESM |
    84→| UI framework | Mantine 7 | Complete component library, responsive props, theming |
    85→| GraphQL client | urql + gql.tada | Lightweight, schema-aware typing, compile-time query validation |
    86→| State | Zustand (planned), React state (current) | Simple global state when needed, component-local by default |
    87→| Forms | React Hook Form + Zod | Schema validation, minimal re-renders |
    88→| Testing | Go: stdlib testing; Web: Vitest + happy-dom | Standard toolchains, fast execution |
    89→
    90→---
    91→
    92→## 2. API Server
    93→
    94→### Startup sequence (`cmd/server/main.go`)
    95→
    96→1. Load `.env` via godotenv (dev convenience, no-op if absent)
    97→2. Parse env vars into `internal/config.Config` struct
    98→3. Configure `slog` logger (format and level from config)
    99→4. Connect pgxpool with retry → `internal/db.Connect`
   100→5. Select JWT validator: RSAValidator if `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` set, else HMACValidator
   101→6. Build chi router with middleware and routes → `internal/router.New`
   102→7. Start HTTP server; block on SIGINT/SIGTERM; graceful shutdown
   103→
   104→### Package map
   105→
   106→| Package | Purpose |
   107→|---|---|
   108→| `cmd/server` | Entrypoint: env loading, wiring, lifecycle |
   109→| `internal/config` | Env var binding (`caarlos0/env` struct tags), `Addr()`, `LogHandler()` |
   110→| `internal/auth` | `Validator` interface; `HMACValidator` (HS256), `RSAValidator` (RS256) |
   111→| `internal/db` | pgxpool connection with retry; goose embedded migration runner |
   112→| `internal/middleware` | RequestID, Logging (slog), Recovery, Auth |
   113→| `internal/router` | Chi router wiring: routes, middleware, GraphQL handler, MCP mount |
   114→| `internal/handler` | HTTP handlers: `Health`, `ExportCSV` |
   115→| `internal/search` | Field registry, field types, SQL generation, validation |
   116→| `internal/mcp` | Model Context Protocol server and tool implementations |
   117→| `internal/server` | `http.Server` with production timeouts; `Shutdown` helper |
   118→| `internal/testdb` | Integration test database helpers |
   119→| `internal/version` | `Version` and `BuildTime` injected via ldflags |
   120→| `graph/schema/` | GraphQL SDL files (source of truth) |
   121→| `graph/scalar/` | Custom scalars (`FilterValue`) |
   122→| `graph/generated/` | gqlgen output — never edit |
   123→| `graph/model/` | Generated Go types — never edit |
   124→| `graph/resolver/` | Hand-written resolver implementations |
   125→| `migrations/` | Goose SQL files + `//go:embed` wrapper |
   126→
   127→### Middleware stack
   128→
   129→Every request passes through (in order):
   130→
   131→```
   132→RequestID → Logging → Recovery → chi.RealIP → chi.StripSlashes → [Auth]
   133→```
   134→
   135→Auth is applied per-route-group, not globally.
   136→
   137→### Routes
   138→
   139→| Method | Path | Auth | Handler |
   140→|---|---|---|---|
   141→| POST | `/graphql` | Public | gqlgen GraphQL |
   142→| GET | `/graphql` | Public | gqlgen GraphQL (GET transport) |
   143→| GET | `/playground` | Public | GraphQL Playground (if `GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND=true`) |
   144→| GET | `/api/export/csv` | Public | CSV export |
   145→| GET | `/private/healthcheck` | Public | DB ping + version JSON |
   146→| * | `/mcp` | Public | MCP streamable HTTP (if `MCP_ENABLED=true`) |
   147→
   148→### Configuration
   149→
   150→All configuration via environment variables:
   151→
   152→| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
   153→|---|---|---|---|
   154→| `DATABASE_URL` | Yes | — | PostgreSQL connection string |
   155→| `JWT_SECRET` | Yes* | — | HS256 signing key (*or `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY`) |
   156→| `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` | No | — | RS256 PEM (takes precedence over `JWT_SECRET`) |
   157→| `JWT_ISSUER` | No | — | Expected JWT issuer claim |
   158→| `JWT_AUDIENCE` | No | — | Expected JWT audience claim |
   159→| `OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL` | No | — | OIDC discovery endpoint (future) |
   160→| `HOST` | No | `0.0.0.0` | Bind address |
   161→| `PORT` | No | `8081` | API listen port |
   162→| `ENV` | No | `development` | Environment name |
   163→| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `info` | trace/debug/info/warn/error |
   164→| `LOG_FORMAT` | No | `json` | `json` or `text` |
   165→| `CORS_ORIGINS` | No | — | Comma-separated allowed origins |
   166→| `GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND` | No | `false` | Enable Playground UI |
   167→| `GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION` | No | `true` | Enable introspection |
   168→| `MCP_ENABLED` | No | `true` | Enable MCP server |
   169→| `SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT` | No | `15s` | Graceful shutdown deadline |
   170→| `MAX_EXPORT_ROWS` | No | `500` | CSV export row limit |
   171→
   172→---
   173→
   174→## 3. Database
   175→
   176→### PostgreSQL with PostGIS
   177→
   178→PostgreSQL 15+ with PostGIS for geographic queries. The database uses multiple
   179→schemas to separate concerns:
   180→
   181→| Schema | Purpose | Key tables |
   182→|---|---|---|
   183→| `search_base` | Core institutional data | `institution`, `dataset`, `annual_dataset`, `dataset_merged` (mat. view), `ranking`, `conference`, `cip_data`, `institution_alias` |
   184→| `location` | Geographic data | `state`, `zip_coordinates`, `institution_normalized_alias` |
   185→| `subscription` | Accounts and billing (planned) | `team`, `team_member`, `subscription`, `subscription_slot`, `purchase` |
   186→| `ugc` | User-generated content (planned) | `saved_institution`, `saved_search` |
   187→| `internal` | System | `event`, `user_feedback` |
   188→
   189→### Institution data model
   190→
   191→Each institution (~6,000 Title IV schools) stored in `search_base.institution`:
   192→
   193→**Fixed columns**: `unit_id` (PK, IPEDS ID), `ope6_id`, `ope8_id`,
   194→`institution_name`, `city`, `state`, `coordinates` (PostGIS geography)
   195→
   196→**JSONB columns** for flexible storage:
   197→
   198→| Column | Contents |
   199→|---|---|
   200→| `nces` | Demographics, test scores, costs, enrollment, financial aid |
   201→| `rankings` | By publication, year, and category |
   202→| `weather` | Köppen climate classification |
   203→| `athletics` | NCAA division, conference, sports |
   204→| `admissions` | Application platform membership |
   205→| `fields_of_study` | CIP codes, credential levels, enrollment |
   206→| `misc` | Partisan lean, AAU membership, metro area, locale |
   207→| `safety_and_security` | Clery Act crime counts |
   208→
   209→### Time-series data
   210→
   211→Annual snapshots (costs, enrollment, outcomes) stored in
   212→`search_base.annual_dataset` with `(unit_id, datakey, year)` keys. A separate
   213→`search_base.dataset` table holds non-temporal data with `(unit_id, datakey)` keys.
   214→
   215→### Materialized views
   216→
   217→**`search_base.dataset_merged`** — The primary search target. Joins
   218→`institution` with the most recent 5 years of `annual_dataset` and all
   219→`dataset` records into a denormalized row per institution. Includes:
   220→
   221→- Core columns: `unit_id`, `institution_name`, `city`, `state`, `coordinates`, `latitude`, `longitude`
   222→- `data` JSONB: Merged aggregate of all JSONB columns and dataset values
   223→- `name_search_vector`: Full-text TSVector on institution name
   224→- `name_location_search_vector`: Full-text TSVector on name + city + state
   225→
   226→**`search_base.dataset_latest`** — Most recent year per datakey per institution.
   227→
   228→### Indexes
   229→
   230→| Type | Target | Purpose |
   231→|---|---|---|
   232→| GIN | `dataset_merged.data` | Fast JSONB containment queries for all filters |
   233→| GIN | `name_search_vector` | Full-text search on name |
   234→| GIN | `name_location_search_vector` | Full-text search on name + location |
   235→| SP-GiST | `coordinates` | Geographic proximity (ST_DWithin) |
   236→| Unique | `unit_id`, `ope6_id`, `ope8_id` | Data integrity |
   237→
   238→### Migrations
   239→
   240→Goose SQL files in `api/migrations/`, embedded via `migrations/migrations.go`
   241→(`//go:embed` in a separate package because embed cannot reference parent paths).
   242→
   243→Current migrations:
   244→- `00001`: Initial schema — all schemas, enums, tables, indexes, foreign keys
   245→- `00002`: `dataset_merged` materialized view
   246→- `00003`: Extended views (7 additional materialized/regular views)
   247→- `00004`: `state.type` column
   248→- `00005`: `institution.subset` column
   249→
   250→Creating a new migration:
   251→```bash
   252→cd api && make migrate-create NAME=short_description
   253→# edit migrations/NNNN_short_description.sql with -- +goose Up / -- +goose Down
   254→make migrate-up
   255→make schema-gen   # regenerate common/.../schema.sql
   256→```
   257→
   258→---
   259→
   260→## 4. Search Engine
   261→
   262→### Architecture
   263→
   264→The search engine (`api/internal/search/`) translates typed JSON filter payloads
   265→into parameterized SQL WHERE clauses against `dataset_merged`. The system is
   266→self-describing: a central field registry drives query execution, the GraphQL
   267→metadata endpoint, the MCP tool schema, and the frontend filter UI.
   268→
   269→### Field interface
   270→
   271→```go
   272→type Field interface {
   273→    Name() string
   274→    Label() string
   275→    Description() string
   276→    Kind() FacetKind
   277→    Source() FieldSource
   278→    Descriptor() FieldDescriptor
   279→    BuildSQL(rawFilter json.RawMessage, argStart int) (expr string, args []any, err error)
   280→}
   281→```
   282→
   283→Every field implements `BuildSQL`, which returns a parameterized boolean
   284→expression and its arguments. `argStart` is the 1-based placeholder index
   285→(`$1`, `$2`, ...) — each field advances it for the next filter.
   286→
   287→### Field types
   288→
   289→| Type | SQL pattern | Example usage |
   290→|---|---|---|
   291→| `FullTextSearchField` | `tsv @@ plainto_tsquery($N)` | Name search |
   292→| `SubstringField` | `col ILIKE '%' \|\| $N \|\| '%'` | City, conference |
   293→| `SingleEnumField` | `col = $N` | Institution type |
   294→| `MultiEnumField` | `col ?& $N` / `col ?\| $N` | Fields of study (any/all mode) |
   295→| `MultiColumnEnumField` | `col = ANY($N::text[])` | State |
   296→| `NumericRangeField` | `col >= $N AND col <= $M` | Enrollment, tuition, grad rate |
   297→| `RankingField` | Numeric range on JSONB path | US News rank |
   298→| `GeoField` | `ST_DWithin(col, ST_MakePoint($N,$M)::geography, $R)` | Distance from ZIP |
   299→| `ExactStringField` | `col = $N` | Exact match |
   300→| `ExactNumericField` | `col = $N` | Congressional district |
   301→
   302→### Field sources
   303→
   304→| Source | SQL resolution |
   305→|---|---|
   306→| `SourceColumn` | Direct column reference |
   307→| `SourceTSVector` | PostgreSQL TSVector column |
   308→| `SourceJSONBPath` | `data->>'key'` or `data->'nested'->'key'` |
   309→| `SourceGeo` | PostGIS geography column |
   310→
   311→### Field metadata
   312→
   313→Every field carries metadata controlling its behavior across all clients:
   314→
   315→| Property | Values | Effect |
   316→|---|---|---|
   317→| `Visibility` | Visible / Restricted / Hidden | Appears in UI and metadata endpoints |
   318→| `Searchability` | Searchable / Restricted / NotSearchable | Usable as a filter |
   319→| `Exportable` | true / false | Included in CSV exports |
   320→| `Sortable` | true / false | Results can be ordered by this field |
   321→| `Theme` | One of 18 themes | Groups the field in UI sidebar |
   322→
   323→### Query execution flow
   324→
   325→1. Client sends `filters[]` (field name + raw JSON value), pagination, sort, optional user location
   326→2. `ValidateRequest` checks all filters against registry, validates bounds and coordinates
   327→3. `BuildCountQuery` assembles `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dataset_merged WHERE ...`
   328→4. `BuildQuery` assembles `SELECT ... FROM dataset_merged WHERE ... ORDER BY ... LIMIT ... OFFSET ...`
   329→5. Each filter's `BuildSQL` contributes a parameterized fragment; fragments are AND-joined
   330→6. If user location provided, distance is computed as a virtual column via `ST_Distance`
   331→7. Results mapped to GraphQL response types
   332→
   333→### Registry
   334→
   335→All fields registered in `registry.go → NewRegistry()`. The registry provides:
   336→
   337→- `Get(name)` — look up a field by name
   338→- `All()` — iterate all fields (for metadata endpoints)
   339→- `Descriptors()` — all field metadata (for MCP tools)
   340→
   341→Adding a field: add `r.MustRegister(...)` in `NewRegistry()` with the appropriate
   342→field type, source, metadata, and theme.
   343→
   344→---
   345→
   346→## 5. GraphQL API
   347→
   348→### Schema-first with gqlgen
   349→
   350→SDL files in `api/graph/schema/` are the source of truth. Running
   351→`make api-generate` produces:
   352→
   353→- `graph/generated/generated.go` — executable schema runtime
   354→- `graph/model/models_gen.go` — Go struct types
   355→
   356→Hand-written code:
   357→- `graph/resolver/resolver.go` — Resolver struct with DB and Registry deps
   358→- `graph/resolver/schema.resolvers.go` — Query implementations
   359→- `graph/resolver/search.resolvers.go` — Search resolver
   360→- `graph/resolver/search_fields.resolvers.go` — Field metadata resolvers
   361→- `graph/scalar/` — Custom scalar marshaling
   362→
   363→### Current queries
   364→
   365→| Query | Returns |
   366→|---|---|
   367→| `search(filters, limit, offset, sort, userLocation)` | `SearchResponse` — paginated results with total count |
   368→| `searchFields` | `[SearchField!]!` — all filterable fields with metadata (interface with 6 concrete types) |
   369→| `searchOutputFields` | `[SearchOutputField!]!` — available result columns |
   370→| `institution(unitID)` | `InstitutionDetail` — full institution data |
   371→| `resolveZip(zip)` | `ZipLocation` — coordinates for a ZIP code |
   372→| `echo(message)` | `EchoResponse` — health check |
   373→
   374→### Key types
   375→
   376→**`SearchInstitution`** — result row with nested objects:
   377→- `InstitutionLocation`: locale, region, metroArea, metroDistance
   378→- `InstitutionAcademics`: type, degree, enrollment, faculty metrics, flags
   379→- `InstitutionCost`: netPrice, tuition, roomAndBoard, aid rates, endowment
   380→- `InstitutionOutcomes`: admission, graduation, retention, transfer rates
   381→- `InstitutionRankings`: per-publication ranks and scores
   382→- `InstitutionAdmissions`: application platforms
   383→- `InstitutionAthletics`: NCAA division, conference
   384→- `InstitutionClimate`: Köppen classification
   385→- `InstitutionCulture`: partisan lean
   386→
   387→**`SearchField`** — interface with concrete types:
   388→`TextSearchField`, `BinarySearchField`, `ChoiceSearchField`,
   389→`NumericalSearchField`, `NumericalRangeSearchField`, `GeoSearchField`
   390→
   391→### Planned queries and mutations
   392→
   393→See [PRODUCT.md](PRODUCT.md) for the full planned feature surface. Key additions:
   394→
   395→- `savedInstitutions`, `savedSearches`, `blockList` (queries)
   396→- `saveInstitution`, `saveSearch`, `blockInstitution` (mutations)
   397→- `compareInstitutions`, `institutionHistory`, `similarInstitutions` (queries)
   398→- `preferences` / `updatePreferences` (query + mutation for user settings)
   399→- `userNotifications` (query for in-app notification center)
   400→
   401→---
   402→
   403→## 6. MCP Server
   404→
   405→### Implementation
   406→
   407→Stateless streamable-HTTP handler at `/mcp` using
   408→`github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp`. Created in
   409→`internal/mcp/server.go`, mounted by `internal/router` when `MCP_ENABLED=true`.
   410→
   411→### Tools
   412→
   413→| Tool | Input | Output |
   414→|---|---|---|
   415→| `search_colleges` | `{filters, limit, offset}` | `{results[], total, limit, offset}` — each result has unitID, name, city, state, lat, lon |
   416→| `get_search_fields` | *(none)* | Array of field descriptors (name, label, description, kind, options) |
   417→| `get_college_detail` | `{unit_id}` | Full institution record with raw JSONB data |
   418→
   419→### Client configuration
   420→
   421→```bash
   422→# Claude Code
   423→claude mcp add kapow --transport http http://localhost:8081/mcp
   424→
   425→# Claude Desktop — add to claude_desktop_config.json
   426→{ "mcpServers": { "kapow": { "url": "http://localhost:8081/mcp" } } }
   427→```
   428→
   429→### WebMCP
   430→
   431→Browser-side MCP widget (`web/src/hooks/useWebMCP.ts`) that initializes
   432→`window.WebMCP` on mount. Registers `search_colleges` and `get_search_fields`
   433→tools that call the GraphQL API directly (no MCP HTTP transport needed in-browser).
   434→
   435→### Planned tools
   436→
   437→**Search enhancements**:
   438→`compare_colleges`, `find_similar_colleges`, `get_institution_trends`
   439→
   440→**Authenticated personalization** (requires JWT in MCP session):
   441→- `save_college` — bookmark an institution with optional notes, rating, tags
   442→- `get_saved_colleges` — retrieve user's bookmarked list
   443→- `update_saved_college` — update notes, rating, priority, intent
   444→- `remove_saved_college` — remove a bookmark
   445→- `get_preferences` / `set_preferences` — read/write user settings (home state, home location)
   446→
   447→These tools allow chatbot interactions like "remember this school", "make Stanford a high priority", and "set my home state to California".
   448→

   451→## 7. Web Application
   452→
   453→### Technology
   454→
   455→| Concern | Library |
   456→|---|---|
   457→| Build | Vite 5 |
   458→| Framework | React 18 + TypeScript 5 (strict) |
   459→| Routing | React Router v6 |
   460→| GraphQL | urql (cache + fetch), gql.tada + @0no-co/graphqlsp |
   461→| UI | Mantine 7 |
   462→| Forms | React Hook Form + Zod |
   463→| State | Zustand (planned), React useState (current) |
   464→| Icons | lucide-react |
   465→| Testing | Vitest + happy-dom (80% thresholds) |
   466→| Linting | ESLint 9 (zero warnings), Prettier |
   467→
   468→### Directory structure
   469→
   470→```
   471→web/src/
   472→├── assets/styles/index.css   # CSS custom properties, global reset
   473→├── components/
   474→│   ├── layout/               # Header (sticky nav), Footer
   475→│   ├── ui/                   # Button, Card, Input, Badge (Mantine wrappers)
   476→│   └── search/               # FilterControl, LocationInput, InstitutionCard,
   477→│                              # InstitutionDetail, ResultsList, ResultsTable,
   478→│                              # ColumnPicker, ViewToggle, SampleSearchCarousel
   479→├── hooks/useWebMCP.ts        # WebMCP initialization
   480→├── pages/
   481→│   ├── Home.tsx              # Hero, quick search, sample searches, features
   482→│   ├── Search.tsx            # Full faceted search page
   483→│   └── About.tsx             # Mission, features, data sources
   484→├── test/                     # Vitest setup, renderWithMantine helper
   485→├── App.tsx                   # React Router routes
   486→├── main.tsx                  # Providers: Mantine, urql, BrowserRouter
   487→└── theme.ts                  # Brand colors, fonts
   488→```
   489→
   490→### Design system
   491→
   492→**Brand colors** (Mantine 10-shade tuples):
   493→- `brand`: deep blue (`#0e1f40` at shade 9) — primary
   494→- `accent`: gold (`#c9a227` at shade 5)
   495→
   496→**CSS custom properties** (`src/assets/styles/index.css`):
   497→- `--color-primary: #0e1f40`
   498→- `--color-accent: #c9a227`
   499→- `--color-primary-foreground: #ffffff`
   500→- `--color-accent-foreground: #0e1f40`
   501→
   502→**Typography**: Inter with system font fallback.
   503→
   504→**Styling**: Mantine style props and inline styles. No Tailwind. PostCSS with
   505→`postcss-preset-mantine` for Mantine's CSS.
   506→
   507→### Search page architecture
   508→
   509→The Search page (`src/pages/Search.tsx`) is the most complex component:
   510→
   511→- **URL-driven state**: All search state (filters, pagination, view mode,
   512→  columns) serialized to URL params. URL is the source of truth.
   513→- **Filter sidebar**: `FilterControl` components grouped by theme. Text fields
   514→  debounced at 400ms. Multi-select checkboxes with search filter for 6+ options.
   515→  Location input with ZIP resolution and browser geolocation.
   516→- **Results area**: Card/List/Table views (persisted in localStorage). Column
   517→  picker (persisted in localStorage). Pagination (5–100 per page). Sort by name
   518→  or distance.
   519→- **GraphQL queries**: `SearchFieldsQuery` and `SearchOutputFieldsQuery` (load
   520→  once on mount), `SearchQuery` (fires on filter/pagination/sort change).
   521→- **Responsive**: Sidebar collapses to toggle on mobile. Full-screen modals on
   522→  small screens.
   523→
   524→### Dev server proxy
   525→
   526→Vite dev server on `:8080` proxies to the API:
   527→```
   528→/graphql → http://localhost:8081
   529→/api     → http://localhost:8081
   530→```
   531→
   532→### Schema sync
   533→
   534→`web/schema.graphql` and `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts` are generated from the API
   535→SDL. After any schema change:
   536→```bash
   537→make web-schema-sync
   538→```
   539→
   540→Both files are committed. `graphql-env.d.ts` enables gql.tada's compile-time
   541→query type checking.
   542→
   543→---
   544→
   545→## 8. Authentication
   546→
   547→### Current implementation
   548→
   549→JWT validation is fully implemented in the API:
   550→
   551→```go
   552→type Validator interface {
   553→    Validate(tokenString string) (*Claims, error)
   554→}
   555→
   556→type Claims struct {
   557→    jwt.RegisteredClaims
   558→    Email      string   `json:"email,omitempty"`
   559→    Roles      []string `json:"roles,omitempty"`
   560→    TeamID     string   `json:"team_id,omitempty"`
   561→    ClientType string   `json:"client_type,omitempty"` // web|cli|mobile|desktop
   562→}
   563→```
   564→
   565→- **HMACValidator**: HS256 using `JWT_SECRET`
   566→- **RSAValidator**: RS256 using `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` (PEM)
   567→- `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` takes precedence when both are set
   568→- Auth middleware reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, validates, stores
   569→  `Claims` in request context
   570→- Context helpers: `SetClaims()`, `GetClaims()`, `MustGetClaims()`
   571→
   572→### Planned: OIDC integration
   573→
   574→External identity provider (Auth0, Firebase, Clerk, or similar). The API
   575→validates JWTs issued by the provider — no password storage in Kapow.
   576→
   577→Config: `OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL` env var for auto-discovery of JWKS endpoint.
   578→
   579→### Account model (planned)
   580→
   581→```
   582→User (from OIDC provider)
   583→ └── Team
   584→      ├── TeamMember (role: owner | member | observer)
   585→      ├── SavedInstitution[]
   586→      ├── SavedSearch[]
   587→      └── Subscription
   588→```
   589→
   590→Database tables already exist in `subscription.*` and `ugc.*` schemas.
   591→
   592→---
   593→
   594→## 9. Data Pipeline
   595→
   596→### Current state
   597→
   598→Fixture data loaded from compressed COPY-format SQL files in
   599→`common/src/main/resources/data/`:
   600→
   601→| File | Contents |
   602→|---|---|
   603→| `complete.sql.xz` | Full dataset (~6,000 institutions) |
   604→| `subset-VT.sql.xz` | Vermont only (fast loading for tests) |
   605→| `subset-DE_MA_VT.sql.xz` | Delaware + Massachusetts + Vermont |
   606→
   607→The `tools/samplesql` Go module extracts deterministic, FK-safe samples from a
   608→live database:
   609→
   610→```bash
   611→cd tools/samplesql
   612→go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --limit 100 --seed 42 --output sample.sql
   613→```
   614→
   615→Extraction order (FK-safe): lookup tables first (state, ranking, conference,
   616→cip_data), then institution-filtered tables (institution, dataset,
   617→annual_dataset, institution_alias, zip_coordinates).
   618→
   619→### Import scripts (from previous version)
   620→
   621→The previous Calypso version had TypeScript importers for each data source
   622→(NCES, rankings, NCAA, weather, crime, etc.) that read source files, normalize
   623→institution names via alias matching, and upsert into PostgreSQL. These will be
   624→ported to Go modules under `tools/`.
   625→
   626→### Planned pipeline
   627→
   628→```
   629→Source files (CSV/JSON/TSV)
   630→        ↓
   631→Go importer (per source)         # tools/<source>-importer/
   632→        ↓
   633→Normalize names (alias matching) # match against institution_alias
   634→        ↓
   635→Upsert into PostgreSQL           # parameterized SQL, pgx
   636→        ↓
   637→Validate (row counts, nulls)     # post-import checks
   638→        ↓
   639→REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW        # CONCURRENTLY (no downtime)
   640→        ↓
   641→Extract fixtures                 # tools/samplesql → common/.../data/
   642→```
   643→
   644→**Materialized view refresh**: `REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY` avoids
   645→locking the view during refresh, keeping search available. Takes 30–60 seconds
   646→on the full dataset.
   647→
   648→---
   649→
   650→## 10. Infrastructure and Deployment
   651→
   652→### Deployment options
   653→
   654→| Option | Components | Best for |
   655→|---|---|---|
   656→| **All-in-one Docker** | Single container: PG + API + nginx + fixtures | Demo, testing, single machine |
   657→| **Docker Compose** | Separate PG, API, nginx containers | Small-scale production |
   658→| **Kubernetes** | API deployment (scalable), managed PG, ingress | Production at scale |
   659→| **Managed services** | Cloud PG (RDS/Cloud SQL), API on Cloud Run/ECS, CDN | Easiest production path |
   660→| **Bare metal / VM** | Direct install | Self-hosted |
   661→
   662→### All-in-one Docker image
   663→
   664→Multi-stage build:
   665→
   666→1. **Go builder**: Compiles `kapow-api` binary (CGO_ENABLED=0, static linking)
   667→2. **Node builder**: `pnpm build` → production frontend assets
   668→3. **Runtime** (`postgis/postgis:15-3.4-alpine`):
   669→   - Installs nginx, supervisord, xz
   670→   - Copies binary, web assets, config, fixture data
   671→   - Entrypoint: init PG, load fixtures on first boot, hand off to supervisord
   672→
   673→**supervisord** manages three processes:
   674→- `postgres` (priority 10)
   675→- `kapow-api` (priority 20, up to 30 restarts)
   676→- `nginx` (priority 20)
   677→
   678→**nginx**: Serves frontend from `/var/www/kapow`, proxies `/graphql` and `/api/`
   679→to `localhost:8081`, SPA fallback for unknown routes.
   680→
   681→**Ports**: 80 (nginx), 8081 (API direct)
   682→
   683→### Development environment
   684→
   685→```bash
   686→make dev   # starts Docker PG (port 15432) + air (API hot-reload) + Vite (web HMR)
   687→```
   688→
   689→Also available as a VS Code devcontainer (`.devcontainer/`) with PostgreSQL 17 +
   690→PostGIS in-container, Go tools (air, golangci-lint), Node 22 + pnpm 9, and
   691→Claude Code.
   692→
   693→### Port map
   694→
   695→| Port | Service | Context |
   696→|---|---|---|
   697→| 80 | nginx | All-in-one Docker |
   698→| 8080 | Vite dev server | Local development |
   699→| 8081 | Go API | All contexts |
   700→| 15432 | PostgreSQL (host-side) | `make dev` |
   701→| 5432 | PostgreSQL | Inside devcontainer |
   702→| 6006 | Storybook | Component development |
   703→
   704→### Planned: Monitoring
   705→
   706→| Concern | Approach |
   707→|---|---|
   708→| Health | `/private/healthcheck` (DB ping, version, latency) |
   709→| Metrics | Prometheus: request latency, error rates, DB pool stats, query times |
   710→| Logging | Structured JSON (slog) → aggregator (CloudWatch, Datadog, Loki) |
   711→| Tracing | OpenTelemetry: request lifecycle, DB queries, search execution |
   712→| Alerts | p99 latency, error rate, pool exhaustion, view staleness |
   713→| Analytics | PostHog or similar: page views, search patterns, conversion |
   714→

   716→
   717→## 11. Performance
   718→
   719→### Targets
   720→
   721→| Metric | Target |
   722→|---|---|
   723→| Search query (p50) | < 100 ms |
   724→| Search query (p99) | < 500 ms |
   725→| Institution detail | < 50 ms |
   726→| Time to first contentful paint | < 1.5 s |
   727→| Time to interactive | < 3 s |
   728→| Lighthouse score | > 90 |
   729→
   730→### Current optimizations
   731→
   732→- **Materialized view**: Pre-joined data eliminates runtime joins
   733→- **GIN indexes**: Fast JSONB and full-text queries
   734→- **SP-GiST index**: Efficient geographic proximity
   735→- **Parameterized SQL**: No query plan cache pollution
   736→- **pgxpool**: Connection pooling
   737→- **Vite production build**: Tree-shaken, code-split, gzipped
   738→
   739→### Planned optimizations
   740→
   741→| Optimization | Impact |
   742→|---|---|
   743→| Redis query cache | TTL-based, invalidated on data refresh |
   744→| CDN for frontend | Edge-cached static assets |
   745→| HTTP cache headers | Cache metadata and detail responses |
   746→| PgBouncer | External connection pooler for high concurrency |
   747→| Pre-computed results | Common queries (home page samples) served from cache |
   748→

   750→
   751→## 12. Testing
   752→
   753→### Test tiers
   754→
   755→| Tier | Command | Time | External deps |
   756→|---|---|---|---|
   757→| Quick | `make test-quick` | ~10–20s | None |
   758→| Standard | `make test-standard` | ~30–60s | None |
   759→| Full | `make test-full` | ~2–5 min | DB + services |
   760→
   761→### Go testing
   762→
   763→- **Unit** (`make api-test-unit`): `-race -short`, no DB. Use `testing.Short()` guards.
   764→- **Integration** (`make test-integration`): `-race -run Integration`, skip if
   765→  `testing.Short()` or `DATABASE_URL` unset.
   766→- **Coverage target**: ≥ 80% per package. `internal/search` ≥ 90%, `graph/scalar` 100%.
   767→  Wiring packages (`cmd/server`, `router`) exempt.
   768→
   769→Integration test pattern:
   770→```go
   771→func IntegrationSearch_MyQuery(t *testing.T) {
   772→    if testing.Short() {
   773→        t.Skip("integration test")
   774→    }
   775→    dsn := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL")
   776→    if dsn == "" {
   777→        t.Skip("DATABASE_URL not set")
   778→    }
   779→    // ...
   780→}
   781→```
   782→
   783→### Web testing
   784→
   785→- **Unit** (`pnpm test`): Vitest + happy-dom, 80% coverage thresholds
   786→- `renderWithMantine()` helper wraps components in MantineProvider
   787→- New `ui/` components require a `.test.tsx` alongside them
   788→- Mock `useQuery` by dispatching on operation name from the AST
   789→
   790→### Build verification
   791→
   792→Every frontend change must pass:
   793→```bash
   794→pnpm build   # tsc -b && vite build — zero errors
   795→pnpm lint    # eslint --max-warnings 0 — zero warnings
   796→```
   797→
   798→### Playwright demo
   799→
   800→14-step walkthrough doubling as a smoke test (`pnpm demo:test`). Covers quick
   801→search, filters, column picker, pagination, sample searches, responsive views.

1→# shatter-core
     2→
     3→Rust core engine (library crate). Contains the concolic explorer, constraint solver integration, protocol types, batch analysis, call graph, and export logic.
     4→
     5→## Key Modules
     6→
     7→- `explorer.rs` — Concolic exploration loop and `format_exploration_report()`
     8→- `protocol.rs` — Protocol message types shared across frontends
     9→- `batch_analyze.rs` — Multi-file analysis orchestration
    10→- `call_graph.rs` — Function dependency graph
    11→- `export.rs` — Test generation from behavior maps
    12→- `invariants.rs` — Invariant inference from execution traces
    13→- `spec.rs` — Behavioral specification output format
    14→- `input_gen.rs` — Generator-aware input generation and mutation
    15→- `coverage_metrics.rs` — Concolic coverage reporting
    16→- `equivalence.rs` — Equivalence class clustering
    17→- `orchestrator.rs` — Multi-round exploration orchestration
    18→- `scan_orchestrator.rs` — Multi-file scan coordination
    19→- `executability.rs` — Opaque type detection and executability checks
    20→
    21→## Property-Based Testing Policy (proptest)
    22→
    23→Every non-trivial public function should have proptest coverage. Prioritize:
    24→
    25→1. **Trust boundaries first**: serialization roundtrips, FFI bridges (Z3 solver), protocol parsing. These have the highest bug density.
    26→2. **Semantic invariants over structural checks**: "solved values match ParamInfo types" is valuable; "Request serializes to JSON" is table stakes. Both are needed, but semantic properties catch real bugs.
    27→3. **Full pipeline properties**: test compose functions, not just units. `arbitrary constraints → solve → overlay → type-check output` catches integration bugs that per-function tests miss.
    28→4. **State machine properties**: for stateful components (orchestrator worklist, coverage tracking), test ordering, deduplication, convergence, and budget exhaustion.
    29→5. **Mutation contracts**: all input generation (mutate, crossover, shrink) must preserve type contracts and vector length.
    30→
    31→Shared generators live in `test_arbitraries.rs` — reuse them. Depth-bound recursive types (SymExpr, TypeInfo) to avoid explosion. Filter NaN from floats (breaks PartialEq roundtrips).
    32→
    33→**When NOT to use proptest**: simple getters/setters, thin wrappers, tests where specific examples are clearer.
    34→
    35→**Target**: every module with a `proptest!` block should have properties covering its core invariants, not just serialization.
    36→
    37→## Contracts Policy (`contracts` crate)
    38→
    39→Contracts (`#[requires]`, `#[ensures]`) are active only in debug/test builds. They are for invariants where **all three** of the following hold:
    40→
    41→1. **Trust boundary**: the function receives data from outside Rust's type system — Z3 FFI, deserialized JSON from a frontend subprocess, or index/length relationships between two independently-constructed collections.
    42→2. **Type gap**: the invariant cannot be encoded in the function signature. Prefer `Option`, `Result`, `NonZeroU32`, enums, and newtypes. If you can express it as a type, do that instead.
    43→3. **Silent corruption**: violating the invariant doesn't produce an immediate, obvious error (panic, Z3 sort mismatch) but instead causes a wrong result that propagates downstream — wrong Z3 sort inferred, solved value with wrong type, truncated output vector.
    44→
    45→**Practical rule**: if you can't point to a real or plausible bug the contract would catch that proptest wouldn't, skip it. Proptest generates thousands of structured inputs and exercises contracts on every call path it reaches. Contracts add value only at boundaries where the corruption is silent enough that even a proptest failure would be hard to diagnose without the contract's pinpointed assertion.
    46→
    47→**Qualifying sites** (the full list — expand only if a new boundary meeting all three criteria is introduced):
    48→
    49→| Function | Why it qualifies |
    50→|----------|-----------------|
    51→| `solve_for_new_path()` | Z3 FFI; negate_index is runtime; out-of-bounds → wrong constraint negated silently |
    52→| `solve_for_new_path()` | Z3 FFI; solved value types vs ParamInfo; Int solution for string param → silent type mismatch |
    53→| `overlay_solved_values()` | Cross-collection alignment; length mismatch → truncated/padded inputs sent to frontend |
    54→| `to_z3_expr()` | Z3 FFI; Param indices are runtime; invalid index → wrong Z3 variable → silently wrong solution |
    55→| Protocol validation wrappers | Subprocess JSON; semantic validity post-serde; empty branch_path → silent hash collision |
    56→
    57→**What does NOT qualify:**
    58→- Orchestrator state transitions (`worklist.push`, `covered_paths.insert`) — collection API guarantees these
    59→- Pure functions like `infer_sort()` — wrong output is a logic bug, test with proptest
    60→- Postconditions that restate the return type or the obvious effect of a `map`/`collect`
    61→- Any invariant expressible as a type (prefer `NonEmpty<Vec<T>>` over `#[requires(!v.is_empty())]`)
    62→- Anywhere proptest already covers the invariant — contracts add redundant runtime checks
    63→

1→# shatter-cli
     2→
     3→Rust CLI binary (clap). Thin layer that parses arguments and delegates to `shatter-core`.
     4→
     5→## Key Files
     6→
     7→- `src/main.rs` — All CLI commands including `run_explore()`, `run_scan()`, `run_export_tests()`
     8→

1→# shatter-ts
     2→
     3→TypeScript language frontend. Node.js subprocess implementing the JSON-over-stdio protocol.
     4→
     5→## Key Files
     6→
     7→- `src/main.ts` — Entry point, protocol handler
     8→- `src/logger.ts` — pino logger configured for stderr with `[shatter-ts]` prefix, reads `SHATTER_LOG_LEVEL`
     9→
    10→## Instrumentor Parity Contract
    11→
    12→`instrumentor.ts` has two symbolic expression builders that **must handle the same AST node types**:
    13→
    14→| Function | Purpose | Location |
    15→|---|---|---|
    16→| `buildSymExprWithFlow()` | Data flow analysis (tracks variables through assignments) | Lines ~278-352 |
    17→| `buildSymExpr()` | Branch condition analysis (builds SymExpr for Z3) | Lines ~860-951 |
    18→
    19→When adding support for a new AST node type (e.g. `CallExpression`, `ElementAccessExpression`), update **both** functions. If `buildSymExprWithFlow()` returns `{kind: "unknown"}` for a node type that `buildSymExpr()` handles, variables assigned from that expression become invisible to the solver, silently degrading concolic coverage.
    20→
    21→## Property-Based Testing (fast-check)
    22→
    23→Property tests live in `src/property.test.ts` using `fast-check`. Use `fc.letrec` for recursive types (SymExpr, TypeInfo). Priority targets:
    24→
    25→- Protocol message roundtrips (table stakes)
    26→- **SymExpr builder parity**: `buildSymExpr` and `buildSymExprWithFlow` must handle the same AST node types — test that neither returns `unknown` for nodes the other handles
    27→- Instrumentor output structural validity
    28→- Any function processing untrusted protocol input from the core engine
    29→
    30→When adding a new AST node type or protocol message, add corresponding fast-check properties.
    31→
    32→## Timeout Contract
    33→
    34→Execution timeout: 15s default, overridden by `SHATTER_EXEC_TIMEOUT` env var (seconds). See `getExecTimeoutMs()` in `src/executor.ts`.
    35→

1→# shatter-go
     2→
     3→Go language frontend. Go binary subprocess implementing the JSON-over-stdio protocol.
     4→
     5→## Key Files
     6→
     7→- `protocol/handler.go` — Protocol handler, uses `log/slog` for `[shatter-go]` prefixed stderr logging
     8→- `protocol/log.go` — slog configuration: `LevelTrace` constant, `prefixHandler` for `[shatter-go]` format
     9→- `instrument/executor.go` — Function execution and instrumentation
    10→
    11→## Property-Based Testing (rapid + native fuzzing)
    12→
    13→Two complementary approaches:
    14→- **rapid** (`protocol/property_test.go`, `instrument/property_test.go`): semantic property tests — roundtrips, behavioral invariants, idempotency. Use for logical properties.
    15→- **Native fuzzing** (`testing.F` in `*_fuzz_test.go`): byte-level mutation for crash/panic discovery at parsing boundaries. Use for any code that deserializes untrusted input.
    16→
    17→When adding a protocol message type or parsing function, add both: a rapid property test for semantic correctness and a fuzz target for crash resistance. Seed corpus from existing test fixtures.
    18→
    19→## Timeout Contract
    20→
    21→Execution timeout: 5s default, overridden by `SHATTER_EXEC_TIMEOUT` env var (seconds). See `execTimeout()` in `instrument/executor.go`.
    22→

1→## Kapow Monorepo Makefile — delegates to component Makefiles
     2→## Usage: make <target>
     3→
     4→# Load .env if present — makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
     5→-include .env
     6→export VITE_SUPABASE_URL VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
     7→
     8→.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit \
     9→        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean schema-gen \
    10→        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-coverage web-e2e web-screenshots web-storybook \
    11→        web-schema-sync \
    12→        lint audit hadolint \
    13→        smoke-test smoke-test-with-server \
    14→        api-image-build web-image-build test-image-build test-image-run \
    15→        tools-build tools-test \
    16→        dev dev-clean dev-prune dev-db-up dev-db-down \
    17→        compose-up compose-down compose-logs \
    18→        test-quick test-standard test-full test-fixture \
    19→        fixture-sql fixture-json \
    20→        build-full \
    21→        spec-gen \
    22→        demo \
    23→        help
    24→
    25→## api-build: Build the API server binary
    26→api-build:
    27→	$(MAKE) -C api build
    28→
    29→## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
    30→build-full: web-build
    31→	$(MAKE) -C api build-full
    32→
    33→## api-run: Run the API server
    34→api-run:
    35→	$(MAKE) -C api run
    36→
    37→## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
    38→api-generate:
    39→	$(MAKE) -C api generate
    40→
    41→## api-test: Run all API tests
    42→api-test:
    43→	$(MAKE) -C api test
    44→
    45→## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
    46→api-test-unit:
    47→	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit
    48→
    49→## api-lint: Run linter on the API
    50→api-lint:
    51→	$(MAKE) -C api lint
    52→
    53→## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
    54→api-migrate-up:
    55→	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up
    56→
    57→## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
    58→api-migrate-down:

    60→
    61→## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
    62→api-clean:
    63→	$(MAKE) -C api clean
    64→
    65→## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
    66→schema-gen:

    68→
    69→## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
    70→web-install:
    71→	$(MAKE) -C web install
    72→
    73→## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
    74→web-dev:
    75→	$(MAKE) -C web dev
    76→
    77→## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
    78→web-build:
    79→	$(MAKE) -C web build
    80→
    81→## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
    82→web-lint:
    83→	$(MAKE) -C web lint
    84→
    85→## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
    86→web-clean:
    87→	$(MAKE) -C web clean
    88→
    89→## web-test: Run web unit tests
    90→web-test:
    91→	$(MAKE) -C web test
    92→
    93→## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
    94→web-test-coverage:

1→# ─── Kapow API — Environment Variables ────────────────────────────────────────
     2→# Copy this file to .env and fill in real values.
     3→# NEVER commit .env to version control.
     4→
     5→# ─── Server ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     6→HOST=0.0.0.0
     7→PORT=8081
     8→
     9→# ─── Database ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    10→# PostgreSQL connection string (pgx format)
    11→# Required — server will not start without this.
    12→DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/kapow?sslmode=disable
    13→# For `make dev-db-up` (standalone dev database on port 5433):
    14→# DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable
    15→
    16→# ─── Authentication (JWT) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    17→# HS256 shared secret — used when JWT_PUBLIC_KEY is not set (dev / simple clients)
    18→JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret
    19→
    20→# RS256 PEM-encoded public key — if set, overrides JWT_SECRET
    21→# JWT_PUBLIC_KEY=-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
    22→
    23→# Expected "iss" claim in incoming tokens (optional)
    24→# JWT_ISSUER=https://auth.example.com
    25→
    26→# Expected "aud" claims — comma-separated (optional)
    27→# JWT_AUDIENCE=kapow-api
    28→
    29→# ── Supabase shortcut ──
    30→# Use the JWT secret from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API → JWT Secret:
    31→# JWT_SECRET=<your-supabase-jwt-secret>
    32→# JWT_ISSUER=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1
    33→# JWT_AUDIENCE=authenticated
    34→
    35→# Future: OIDC discovery URL (not yet active)
    36→# OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL=https://auth.example.com/.well-known/openid-configuration
    37→
    38→# ─── Supabase (Frontend Auth SDK) ───────────────────────────────────────────
    39→# Project URL and anon key from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API.
    40→# These are used by the Supabase JS SDK in the frontend for login/signup flows.
    41→# SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
    42→# SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...
    43→
    44→# Vite exposes these to the browser (VITE_ prefix required)
    45→# VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
    46→# VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...
    47→
    48→# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    49→LOG_LEVEL=info          # trace | debug | info | warn | error
    50→LOG_FORMAT=json         # json (default/prod) | text (dev)
    51→
    52→# ─── HTTP Debug Logging ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    53→# Request header logging: none (off) | selected | all
    54→# HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS=none
    55→# Comma-separated headers to log when mode is "selected"
    56→# HTTP_DEBUG_SELECTED_HEADERS=Content-Type,Accept,User-Agent
    57→# Redact sensitive headers (Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, X-Api-Key)
    58→# HTTP_DEBUG_REDACT_SENSITIVE=true
    59→# Response logging: none (off) | headers | full (headers + body)
    60→# HTTP_DEBUG_RESPONSE_MODE=none
    61→# Max response body bytes to capture when mode is "full"
    62→# HTTP_DEBUG_BODY_MAX_BYTES=4096
    63→
    64→# ─── Environment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    65→ENV=development         # development | staging | production
    66→
    67→# ─── CORS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→# Comma-separated allowed origins (empty = no CORS headers set)
    69→CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:8080
    70→
    71→# ─── GraphQL ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    72→GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND=true     # Enable Playground UI (dev only — disable in prod)
    73→GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION=true  # Enable schema introspection
    74→
    75→# ─── MCP (Model Context Protocol) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    76→MCP_ENABLED=true               # Enable MCP server endpoint at /mcp
    77→# MCP requires a valid JWT — same tokens used for /graphql work for /mcp.
    78→# OAuth 2.1 Protected Resource Metadata is served at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource
    79→
    80→# ─── OpenTelemetry ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    81→# Set to true to enable OTLP HTTP export of traces and metrics.
    82→OTEL_ENABLED=false
    83→# Standard OTel SDK env vars (only used when OTEL_ENABLED=true):
    84→# OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=https://otlp.example.com:4318
    85→# OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS=x-api-key=YOUR_KEY
    86→# OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=kapow-api   (overridden by code — set in Config)
    87→# See https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/configuration/sdk-environment-variables/
    88→
    89→# ─── Shutdown ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    90→SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT=15s
    91→
    92→# ─── Export ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    93→MAX_EXPORT_ROWS=500                # Maximum rows in CSV export (default: 500)
    94→
    95→# ─── Rate Limiting ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    96→RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true            # Master switch for all rate limits
    97→RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL=60              # /graphql requests per minute (anonymous)
    98→RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH=120        # /graphql requests per minute (authenticated)
    99→RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT=10               # /api/export/csv requests per minute (per user)
   100→RATE_LIMIT_MCP=120                 # /mcp/* requests per minute (per user)
   101→
   102→# ─── Private Endpoint Access ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   103→# Comma-separated CIDRs allowed to access /private/* routes
   104→PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS=127.0.0.0/8,::1/128
   105→

1→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2→# Stage 1: Build the Go API binary
     3→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4→FROM golang:1.24-alpine AS builder-api
     5→
     6→ARG VERSION=docker
     7→ARG BUILD_TIME
     8→
     9→WORKDIR /build
    10→
    11→# Download dependencies first (layer caching)
    12→COPY api/go.mod api/go.sum ./
    13→RUN go mod download
    14→
    15→COPY api/ ./
    16→
    17→RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build \
    18→        -ldflags "-s -w \
    19→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.Version=${VERSION} \
    20→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.BuildTime=${BUILD_TIME}" \
    21→        -o /bin/kapow-api \
    22→        ./cmd/server
    23→
    24→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25→# Stage 2: Build the web frontend
    26→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27→FROM node:22-alpine AS builder-web
    28→
    29→WORKDIR /build
    30→
    31→# Install pnpm then fetch dependencies (layer caching)
    32→RUN npm install -g pnpm@latest
    33→
    34→COPY web/package.json web/pnpm-lock.yaml ./
    35→RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
    36→
    37→COPY web/ ./
    38→
    39→ARG VITE_SUPABASE_URL
    40→ARG VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
    41→RUN pnpm build
    42→
    43→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44→# Stage 3: Runtime image — postgres+postgis, Go API, nginx, supervisord
    45→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46→FROM postgis/postgis:15-3.4-alpine
    47→
    48→# Install runtime deps: nginx (web server + proxy), supervisor (process manager), xz (fixture decompression)
    49→# hadolint ignore=DL3018
    50→RUN apk add --no-cache nginx supervisor xz
    51→
    52→# API binary
    53→COPY --from=builder-api /bin/kapow-api /usr/local/bin/kapow-api
    54→
    55→# Web assets served by nginx
    56→COPY --from=builder-web /build/dist /var/www/kapow
    57→
    58→# Config files
    59→COPY docker/nginx.conf      /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
    60→COPY docker/supervisord.conf /etc/supervisord.conf
    61→COPY docker/entrypoint.sh   /entrypoint.sh
    62→
    63→# Fixture data — loaded on first boot
    64→COPY common/src/main/resources/data/complete.sql.xz /data/complete.sql.xz
    65→
    66→RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh \
    67→    && mkdir -p /run/nginx
    68→
    69→EXPOSE 80
    70→
    71→ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
    72→

# Build Your Open Brain: Complete Setup Guide

This comprehensive guide provides instructions for creating a personal knowledge system that integrates with multiple AI tools through a unified database and retrieval interface.

## System Architecture

The setup consists of two main components:

**Part 1 - Capture:** A Slack channel captures thoughts, which are automatically embedded, classified, and stored in a Supabase database via an Edge Function.

**Part 2 - Retrieval:** An MCP server enables any connected AI tool to search and write to the brain through semantic similarity rather than keyword matching.

## Required Services

All services operate on free tiers:
- **Supabase:** Vector database with search capabilities
- **OpenRouter:** AI gateway providing embeddings and metadata extraction
- **Slack:** Message capture interface

## Cost Structure

For typical usage of 20 thoughts daily, monthly API costs range from approximately $0.10 to $0.30, covering embeddings and metadata extraction services.

## Setup Process

The 13-step installation requires approximately 45 minutes with no coding experience necessary. Users copy and paste configuration code across three services, then deploy Edge Functions to Supabase for message processing and MCP functionality.

## Key Features

The system enables:
- Automatic embedding generation for semantic search
- Metadata extraction identifying people, action items, dates, and topics
- Multi-AI integration (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor)
- Direct thought capture from any MCP-compatible client
- Persistent cross-tool memory without middleware dependencies

The guide emphasizes saving credentials throughout setup and provides troubleshooting guidance for common issues related to Slack connectivity, function deployment, and AI client configuration.

1→//! Protocol types for communication between the Rust core and language frontends.
     2→//!
     3→//! All messages are newline-delimited JSON over stdin/stdout. The core sends
     4→//! [`Request`] messages to frontends and receives [`Response`] messages back.
     5→//! Every message includes a protocol version for compatibility checking.
     6→
     7→use std::collections::HashMap;
     8→
     9→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    10→
    11→use crate::crypto_registry::{CryptoDirection, OutputSemantics, ParamRole};
    12→use crate::execution_record::{
    13→    BranchDecision, ErrorInfo, ExternalCall, SideEffect, SymConstraint, TraceEvent,
    14→    TruncationInfo,
    15→};
    16→use crate::sym_expr::SymExpr;
    17→use crate::types::{ParamInfo, TypeInfo};
    18→
    19→/// Current protocol version.
    20→pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: &str = "0.1.0";
    21→
    22→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23→// Setup lifecycle types
    24→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25→
    26→/// Granularity level for setup/teardown lifecycle management.
    27→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    28→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    29→pub enum SetupLevel {
    30→    Session,
    31→    File,
    32→    Function,
    33→    Execution,
    34→}
    35→
    36→/// A single entry in a setup context stack, associating a lifecycle level
    37→/// with the opaque context value returned by its Setup command.
    38→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    39→pub struct SetupContextEntry {
    40→    pub level: SetupLevel,
    41→    pub context: serde_json::Value,
    42→}
    43→
    44→/// Stack of active setup contexts, ordered from outermost (session) to
    45→/// innermost (execution). Passed to Execute so frontends can restore
    46→/// all active setup state.
    47→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    48→pub struct SetupContextStack {
    49→    pub contexts: Vec<SetupContextEntry>,
    50→}
    51→
    52→impl From<crate::config::SetupMode> for SetupLevel {
    53→    fn from(mode: crate::config::SetupMode) -> Self {
    54→        match mode {
    55→            crate::config::SetupMode::PerFunction => SetupLevel::Function,
    56→            crate::config::SetupMode::PerExecution => SetupLevel::Execution,
    57→        }
    58→    }
    59→}
    60→
    61→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→// Request: Core → Frontend
    63→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→
    65→/// A request message sent from the core engine to a language frontend.
    66→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    67→pub struct Request {
    68→    /// Protocol version for compatibility checking.
    69→    pub protocol_version: String,
    70→    /// Unique identifier for correlating responses to requests.
    71→    pub id: u64,
    72→    /// The command to execute.
    73→    #[serde(flatten)]

    75→}

    77→/// Commands the core can send to a frontend.
    78→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    79→#[serde(tag = "command", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    80→pub enum Command {
    81→    /// Initial handshake to negotiate protocol version and capabilities.
    82→    Handshake {

    84→        capabilities: Vec<String>,
    85→    },
    86→    /// Analyze a function to extract type information, branches, and dependencies.

    90→        /// Name of the function to analyze. If absent, analyze all exported functions.
    91→        function: Option<String>,
    92→        /// Detected project root directory, if any.
    93→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    94→        project_root: Option<String>,
    95→    },
    96→    /// Instrument a function for symbolic constraint tracking.
    97→    Instrument {
    98→        /// Path to the source file.

   100→        /// Name of the function to instrument.
   101→        function: String,
   102→        /// Mock configurations for external dependencies.
   103→        mocks: Vec<MockConfig>,
   104→        /// Detected project root directory, if any.
   105→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]

   107→    },
   108→    /// Execute an instrumented function with specific inputs and mocks.
   109→    Execute {
   110→        /// Fully qualified function identifier.
   111→        function: String,
   112→        /// Input values for the function parameters (JSON-encoded).
   113→        inputs: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
   114→        /// Mock configurations for external dependencies.
   115→        mocks: Vec<MockConfig>,

   120→    /// Run a setup file to initialize state before function execution.

   134→    },
   135→    /// Tear down state established by a prior Setup command.

   142→    /// Invoke a custom generator to produce a value for a type or parameter.

1→# Beads Configuration File
     2→# This file configures default behavior for all bd commands in this repository
     3→# All settings can also be set via environment variables (BD_* prefix)
     4→# or overridden with command-line flags
     5→
     6→# Issue prefix for this repository (used by bd init)
     7→# If not set, bd init will auto-detect from directory name
     8→# Example: issue-prefix: "myproject" creates issues like "myproject-1", "myproject-2", etc.
     9→# issue-prefix: ""
    10→
    11→# Use no-db mode: load from JSONL, write back after each command
    12→# When true, bd will use .beads/issues.jsonl as the source of truth
    13→# instead of the Dolt database
    14→no-db: true
    15→
    16→# Enable JSON output by default
    17→# json: false
    18→
    19→# Default actor for audit trails (overridden by BD_ACTOR or --actor)
    20→# actor: ""
    21→
    22→# Debounce interval for auto-flush (can also use BEADS_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE)
    23→# flush-debounce: "5s"
    24→
    25→# Export events (audit trail) to .beads/events.jsonl on each flush/sync
    26→# When enabled, new events are appended incrementally using a high-water mark.
    27→# Use 'bd export --events' to trigger manually regardless of this setting.
    28→# events-export: false
    29→
    30→# Git branch for beads commits (bd sync will commit to this branch)
    31→# IMPORTANT: Set this for team projects so all clones use the same sync branch.
    32→# This setting persists across clones (unlike database config which is gitignored).
    33→# Can also use BEADS_SYNC_BRANCH env var for local override.
    34→# If not set, bd sync will require you to run 'bd config set sync.branch <branch>'.
    35→# sync-branch: "beads-sync"
    36→
    37→# Multi-repo configuration (experimental - bd-307)
    38→# Allows hydrating from multiple repositories and routing writes to the correct JSONL
    39→# repos:
    40→#   primary: "."  # Primary repo (where this database lives)
    41→#   additional:   # Additional repos to hydrate from (read-only)
    42→#     - ~/beads-planning  # Personal planning repo
    43→#     - ~/work-planning   # Work planning repo
    44→
    45→# Integration settings (access with 'bd config get/set')
    46→# These are stored in the database, not in this file:
    47→# - jira.url
    48→# - jira.project
    49→# - linear.url
    50→# - linear.api-key
    51→# - github.org
    52→# - github.repo

1→package config
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"strings"
     7→	"time"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/caarlos0/env/v11"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// Config holds all application configuration loaded from environment variables.
    13→type Config struct {
    14→	Host string `env:"HOST" envDefault:"0.0.0.0"`
    15→	Port int    `env:"PORT" envDefault:"8081"`
    16→
    17→	DatabaseURL string `env:"DATABASE_URL,required"`
    18→
    19→	JWTSecret    string `env:"JWT_SECRET"` //nolint:gosec // G117: config field name, not a hardcoded secret
    20→	JWTPublicKey string `env:"JWT_PUBLIC_KEY"`
    21→	JWTIssuer    string `env:"JWT_ISSUER"`
    22→	JWTAudience  string `env:"JWT_AUDIENCE"`
    23→
    24→	OIDCDiscoveryURL string `env:"OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL"`
    25→
    26→	LogLevel  string `env:"LOG_LEVEL" envDefault:"info"`
    27→	LogFormat string `env:"LOG_FORMAT" envDefault:"json"`
    28→
    29→	Env string `env:"ENV" envDefault:"development"`
    30→
    31→	CORSOrigins string `env:"CORS_ORIGINS"`
    32→
    33→	GraphQLPlayground    bool `env:"GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND" envDefault:"false"`
    34→	GraphQLIntrospection bool `env:"GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION" envDefault:"true"`
    35→
    36→	MCPEnabled  bool `env:"MCP_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    37→	OTelEnabled bool `env:"OTEL_ENABLED" envDefault:"false"`
    38→
    39→	ShutdownTimeout time.Duration `env:"SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT" envDefault:"15s"`
    40→
    41→	MaxExportRows int `env:"MAX_EXPORT_ROWS" envDefault:"500"`
    42→
    43→	// Rate limiting
    44→	RateLimitEnabled     bool   `env:"RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    45→	RateLimitGraphQL     int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL" envDefault:"60"`
    46→	RateLimitGraphQLAuth int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH" envDefault:"120"`
    47→	RateLimitExport      int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT" envDefault:"10"`
    48→	RateLimitMCP         int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_MCP" envDefault:"120"`
    49→	PrivateAllowlistCIDRs string `env:"PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS" envDefault:"127.0.0.0/8,::1/128"`
    50→
    51→	// HTTP debug logging
    52→	HTTPDebugRequestHeaders  string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS" envDefault:"none"`
    53→	HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_SELECTED_HEADERS" envDefault:"Content-Type,Accept,User-Agent"`
    54→	HTTPDebugRedactSensitive bool   `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_REDACT_SENSITIVE" envDefault:"true"`
    55→	HTTPDebugResponseMode    string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_RESPONSE_MODE" envDefault:"none"`
    56→	HTTPDebugBodyMaxBytes    int    `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_BODY_MAX_BYTES" envDefault:"4096"`
    57→
    58→	// logLevelVar holds the dynamic log level. It is initialized by LogHandler
    59→	// and can be updated at runtime to change the global log level without a restart.
    60→	logLevelVar slog.LevelVar
    61→
    62→	// swappableWriter is the log output target, swappable at runtime.
    63→	swappableWriter *SwappableWriter
    64→
    65→	// categoryHandler wraps the slog handler with per-category level overrides.
    66→	categoryHandler *CategoryHandler
    67→}
    68→
    69→// Load parses and validates configuration from environment variables.
    70→func Load() (*Config, error) {
    71→	cfg := &Config{}
    72→	if err := env.Parse(cfg); err != nil {
    73→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("config: %w", err)
    74→	}
    75→	return cfg, nil
    76→}
    77→
    78→// Addr returns the host:port bind address.
    79→func (c *Config) Addr() string {
    80→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", c.Host, c.Port)
    81→}
    82→
    83→// LevelTrace is a custom log level below DEBUG for very detailed diagnostic
    84→// output. Usage: logger.Log(ctx, config.LevelTrace, "msg", "key", val)
    85→const LevelTrace = slog.Level(-8)
    86→
    87→// ParseLogLevel converts a log level string to the corresponding slog.Level.
    88→func ParseLogLevel(s string) slog.Level {
    89→	switch s {
    90→	case "trace":
    91→		return LevelTrace
    92→	case "debug":
    93→		return slog.LevelDebug
    94→	case "warn":
    95→		return slog.LevelWarn
    96→	case "error":
    97→		return slog.LevelError
    98→	default:
    99→		return slog.LevelInfo
   100→	}

1→# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
     2→
     3→# --- Builder stage ---
     4→FROM rust:1-bookworm AS builder
     5→
     6→ARG NODE_VERSION=22
     7→ARG GO_VERSION=1.23.6
     8→ARG TARGETARCH
     9→
    10→# System deps: libclang for bindgen, cmake for Z3 build, libz3-dev for headers/libs
    11→RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    12→    build-essential \
    13→    pkg-config \
    14→    libclang-dev \
    15→    cmake \
    16→    libz3-dev \
    17→    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
    18→
    19→# Install Node.js (needed by build.rs to bundle shatter-ts)
    20→RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_${NODE_VERSION}.x | bash - \
    21→    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends nodejs \
    22→    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
    23→
    24→# Install Go (needed by build.rs to compile shatter-go)
    25→RUN GOARCH=$(case "${TARGETARCH}" in arm64) echo "arm64" ;; *) echo "amd64" ;; esac) \
    26→    && curl -fsSL "https://go.dev/dl/go${GO_VERSION}.linux-${GOARCH}.tar.gz" | tar -C /usr/local -xz
    27→ENV PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:${PATH}"
    28→
    29→WORKDIR /build
    30→
    31→# Copy dependency manifests first for layer caching
    32→COPY Cargo.toml Cargo.lock ./
    33→COPY shatter-core/Cargo.toml shatter-core/Cargo.toml
    34→COPY shatter-cli/Cargo.toml shatter-cli/Cargo.toml
    35→COPY shatter-ts/package.json shatter-ts/package-lock.json* shatter-ts/
    36→COPY shatter-go/go.mod shatter-go/go.sum* shatter-go/
    37→
    38→# Copy full source
    39→COPY shatter-core/ shatter-core/
    40→COPY shatter-cli/ shatter-cli/
    41→COPY shatter-ts/ shatter-ts/
    42→COPY shatter-go/ shatter-go/
    43→COPY shatter-rust/ shatter-rust/
    44→
    45→# Build Z3 from source instead of using the prebuilt gh-release binary.
    46→# The prebuilt binary requires glibc 2.39 (Ubuntu 24.04), but building from
    47→# source links against the builder's glibc (2.36/Bookworm), which matches
    48→# node:22-slim at runtime — saving ~130MB in image size.
    49→RUN sed -i 's/features = \["gh-release"\]/features = []/' shatter-core/Cargo.toml
    50→
    51→# Build the main CLI (build.rs bundles TS and compiles Go frontend)
    52→RUN cargo build --release -p shatter-cli
    53→
    54→# Build shatter-rust frontend (separate from workspace)
    55→RUN cargo build --release --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml
    56→
    57→# Collect any dynamically-linked Z3 libs for the runtime stage
    58→RUN mkdir -p /build/libs \
    59→    && (ldd target/release/shatter 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '/\S+libz3\S+' | xargs -I{} cp {} /build/libs/ 2>/dev/null || true) \
    60→    && touch /build/libs/.keep
    61→
    62→# --- Runtime stage ---
    63→FROM node:22-slim
    64→
    65→# Copy Z3 shared libs if any were dynamically linked
    66→COPY --from=builder /build/libs/ /usr/local/lib/
    67→RUN ldconfig 2>/dev/null || true; rm -f /usr/local/lib/.keep
    68→
    69→# Copy binaries
    70→COPY --from=builder /build/target/release/shatter /usr/local/bin/shatter
    71→COPY --from=builder /build/shatter-rust/target/release/shatter-rust /usr/local/bin/shatter-rust
    72→
    73→# OCI image labels
    74→LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/shatter-dev/shatter"
    75→LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Automatic exploratory testing via concolic execution"
    76→LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"
    77→
    78→WORKDIR /repo
    79→ENTRYPOINT ["shatter"]
    80→

1→FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/base:ubuntu-24.04
     2→
     3→ARG RUST_VERSION=stable
     4→ARG NODE_VERSION=22
     5→ARG GO_VERSION=1.24.4
     6→ARG GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION=v1.64.8
     7→
     8→# Install OS dependencies (z3 solver libs needed by shatter-core)
     9→RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \

    17→

    28→

    30→USER root

    32→    && apt-get install -y nodejs \
    33→    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
    34→
    35→# Install Claude Code CLI
    36→RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
    37→
    38→# Install Go
    39→RUN curl -fsSL "https://go.dev/dl/go${GO_VERSION}.linux-amd64.tar.gz" | tar -C /usr/local -xz
    40→ENV PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:/home/vscode/go/bin:${PATH}"
    41→
    42→# Install golangci-lint
    43→RUN curl -sSfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/golangci/golangci-lint/HEAD/install.sh \
    44→    | sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin ${GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION}
    45→
    46→# Install beads (bd) issue tracker

    48→
    49→USER vscode
    50→
